LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PARISH MISSION PROPERTY, LLC, SOUL LOUNGE, LLC, JASON PERKINS AND MICHAEL O'CONNOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS DENIED AS TO ALL FOUR DEFENDANTS. THE DECLARATIONS OF RAUL DELEON, PETE LAZETICH AND JOSEPH MAUCH ESTABLISH THAT ALL FOUR MOVING DEFENDANTS WERE PROPERLY SERVED BY SUBSTITUTE SERVICE. THE DECLARATIONS ALSO STRONGLY SUPPORT THE INFERENCE THAT ALL FOUR MOVING DEFENDANTS KNEW ABOUT THE PENDENCY OF THIS CASE IN SUFFICIENT TIME FOR THEM TO RESPOND TO THE SUMMONS AND FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, BUT CHOSE NOT TO DO SO, INSTEAD HOPING THAT THEIR CONDUCT WOULD SOMEHOW EVADE AND AVOID THEIR OBLIGATION TO RESPOND. EVEN IF THE MOVING PAPERS WERE NOT PROCEDURALLY DEFICIENT, THESE DECLARATIONS ARE MORE THAN SUFFICIENT TO DEFEAT ANY LEGAL GROUNDS FOR THE SETTING ASIDE OF THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, EITHER STATUTORY OR IN EQUITY. THE MOVING PAPERS ARE PROCEDURALLY DEFICIENT BECAUSE THEY DO NOT INCLUDE A PROPOSED RESPONSE TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. MOREOVER, THE MOVING PAPERS FAIL TO MAKE ANY SHOWING OF DILIGENCE IN SEEKING TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULTS AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

DEFENDANTS JASON PERKINS, MICHAEL O'CONNOR, SOUL LOUNGE, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is Off calendar per the Withdrawal of Motion filed on September 12, 2017.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default; Mpa Iso Dec
Off Calendar Jul-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
ppearances:
Attorneys, Daniel M. Poniatowski and Joseph V. Mauch, both of the law offices of Shartsis Friese LLP (415.421.6500) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

Attorney, Francis McKeown of the law offices of McKeown & Associates (510.647.0600) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS PARISH MISSION PROPERTY, LLC, SOUL LOUNGE, LLC, JASON PERKINS and MICHAEL O'CONNOR's Motion To Set Aside Default is DENIED as to all four defendants. The declarations of Raul Deleon, Pete Lazetich and Joseph Mauch establish that all four moving defendants were properly served by substitute service. The declarations also strongly support the inference that all four moving defendants knew about the pendency of this case in sufficient time for them to respond to the summons and first amended complaint, but chose not to do so, instead hoping that their conduct would somehow evade and avoid their obligation to respond. Even if the moving papers were not procedurally deficient, these declarations are more than sufficient to defeat any legal grounds for the setting aside of the default and default judgment, either statutory or in equity. The moving papers are procedurally deficient because they do not include a proposed response to the first amended complaint. Moreover, the moving papers fail to make any showing of diligence in seeking to set aside the defaults and default judgment. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
